summary: Rough but listenable matrix recording captures a powerful post‑Pigpen performance highlighted by an epic Other One and soaring Eyes of the World.
review: The show is widely praised for its intense second‑set jams – especially The Other One, Playing in the Band, Eyes of the World and China Doll – with Jerry and Phil delivering standout improvisations.  Recording quality is mixed: the soundboard sections are usable but riddled with splice glitches, occasional channel drops and a very weak audience feed that many deem “only for completists.”

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the musical performance highly (average ~4.2) while consistently noting sub‑par recording quality (~3.0); weighted combination yields a solid 3.8‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard (SBD) portions are listenable but contain splice glitches (especially in Eyes), occasional mono sections and slight vocal distortion; audience (AUD) tracks are described as muddy, with channel drops and harsh patches that make them only tolerable for completists.

poor_songs: Playin' in the Band (over‑extended per one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first show after Pig Pen’s death feels a bit subdued early on, with the band playing in a circle and lacking focus, but the second set erupts into classic 1972‑74 jam energy, delivering long, cohesive improvisations that many fans consider among the year’s best.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for wild, spacey leads on The Other One and a ‘meltdown’ vibe in the jams; one reviewer notes his guitar is weak in the mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for heavy presence on The Other One and animated playing throughout the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard work noted as spacious and complementary to Jerry, with good interplay heard in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Intense drumming praised, especially Bill Kreutzmann’s contributions during the jam sections.
